hypothesis: Training AMDEN on datasets augmented with full annealing trajectories (i.e., time-ordered snapshots across melting and slow cooling) will enable the standard denoising procedure (without HMC) to generate amorphous structures whose geometry-optimized potential energy distribution is statistically indistinguishable from the slow-quenched (anneal) reference dataset by a two-sample Kolmogorov–Smirnov test (p > 0.05).
